images.caption:
Figure 2:Overview of the full pipeline. In post-training, we continue training
on noisy competitive-programming data, perform supervised fine-tuning on
reference solutions, train auxiliary
hypothesis generation policyπhypothesis\pi_{\mathrm{hypothesis}}and summarization policyπsummary\pi_{\mathrm{summary}}and jointly optimize the
system with multi-component RL. At
test/online-contest time, the model uses direct generation for easy cases, and an online
test-time RL loop for harder
cases.

header: 4Agentic GRPO with Immediate Reward and Delayed Correction

images.caption:
Figure 3:Hypothesis generation and small-scale verification.
The agent first proposes a compact characterization
(kmax=maxv⁡(last​(v)−first​(v))k_{\max}=\max_{v}(\mathrm{last}(v)-\mathrm{first}(v))), then
generates small random instances, computes the exact
answer via a brute-force solver that enumerates all tuples(i,j,k)∈S​(b)(i,j,k)\in S(b), and compares against the hypothesized value.
A mismatch triggers hypothesis revision and only validated hypotheses
are promoted to solution synthesis.

header: 7Post Training with Continue Training and SFT

images.caption: Figure 4:Examples of contest figures whose visual structure is difficult to capture with text-only descriptions.

Figure 5:An illustration of pipelined context parallelism for one block
with 3 DeltaNet layers (L1, L2, L3) + 1 softmax attention layer with 4 CP ranks and
4 micro-batches for illustration purposes. In the DeltaNet phase, each GPU processes micro-batches (MB)
in a staggered pipeline, passing the recurrent state forward; startup
and drain bubbles (gray) are confined to the triangular corners.
The softmax attention layer is executed with synchronized
all-to-all communication at full utilization.
